item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"As they are all face-down, you do not recognize the bodies in the water."
absolute gold. take my upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I couldn't understand why the page http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-err0r opens up to a blank page despite being linked in the footnotes. Did that page suppose to say something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've no idea what this is supposed to mean.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1

Jetpack-Cat

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What the "O" in "O5" stands for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"…Overwatch…"

What?

item.title: Mimetic hazards
item.content:encoded:
For anyone who ever wonders what a dangerous meme is…

I was joking with my wife while reading this SCiP and when I got to the part that says "you do not recognize the bodies in the water" my blood turned to ice and my smile vanished on the spot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, like, I can kind of get where people are annoyed with the format of this one. But here's where being an old fart helped me. To me, this reads like an old school, Infocom interactive fiction that's been played out to the finish. I dug the format, and I think if there was some way to work that into the concept (The skip is ACTUALLY an IF in which you're placed in various XK class scenarios and have to try and prevent them, for example) I think the format screw would work a lot better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, like, I can kind of get where people are annoyed with the format of this one. But here's where being an old fart helped me. To me, this reads like an old school, Infocom interactive fiction that's been played out to the finish. I dug the format, and I think if there was some way to work that into the concept (The skip is ACTUALLY an IF in which you're placed in various XK class scenarios and have to try and prevent them, for example) I think the format screw would work a lot better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wiki doubleposting. Ignore.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Finally got around to some minor updates for this to address some of the issues people had regarding its capabilities.

item.title: Community Outreach Team Captain Post
item.content:encoded: As the author has been disqualified for poor sportsmanship, this entry has been retroactively removed from the Jam Contest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely brilliant. You know how to make horrifying scenarios; a scip that takes over all water on the planet and killing all life fits the bill. As people have joked, this is very akin to When Day Breaks, and you've mentioned that was the point, and that 001 proposal is my favorite by far. Looking forward to more apocalyptic scenarios from you; you really know how to capture the terror of inevitability and hopelessness, so keep it up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are the two different pictures significant in some way? I think I'm missing something here, something more than an anomalous murder-molecule replacing all water on the planet.

item.title: Review
item.content:encoded:
Hey Author! As per your request, I've read over the article a few times and found a decent amount of interesting stuff. For clarity's sake I've separated the article's crit into sections about the story, about the skip itself and about some random odds and ends that didn't fit into either. Let's-a go!

+ The Story
- Title for hiding text

The Good: First of all, I'd like to mention that I enjoyed the story a fair bit and that you're a pretty good horror writer. I genuinely felt a lot of tension during the article and the ending was really creepy. I especially liked a lot of the stranger deaths which made me wonder just how powerful this thing is. Fuck, the thing with the flesh-slurry terrified me.

The Bad: That said, the story's been done before. By you, for that matter. It's a similar if slightly altered version of your 001 proposal. The one of the biggest differences is that it's set during a possibly world-ending breach rather than an already ravaged world. The story is semi-standard fare for breach tales (the thing breaks loose, most of the staff is killed in brutal ways) but well written enough that that isn't really a problem.

The biggest issue for me is that the thing that made the 001 proposal so amazing is gone: the characters. Sure, technically the protagonist of that isn't super detailed but at least he seemed a bit more like an actual human being than a reader-projection. This dude is pretty much just like the protagonist of every harem anime ever. Pretty Bland and kinda confused about everything. At the very least 001 had Dr. Igotta for interesting character writing.

In conclusion, the story's a well written and nicely polished collection of fairly common tropes with a couple of nice deaths thrown in as some icing of the decent by the numbers cake.

+ The Skip
- Title for hiding text

The Good: Honestly, there isn't that much to say about the entity itself, it's seems fairly original to me (or at least I haven't seen anything too much like on the site) and I really like the part about the water system even though it's incredibly short. Honestly any meteorology skip tends to be pretty cool to me so points for that.

The Bad: However, those points are taken away fairly quickly because the entity pretty much seemed like a Mary-Sue to me. It didn't have a weakness, it seemed pretty powerful and seemed to have a taste for sadism (judging by some of my favorite deaths and the fact that it seems to prefer live stuff for food). Congrats, you just wrote an anomalous creepypasta antagonist. Meaningless danger but with none of the stakes.

The containment procedures also confused me a bit. First it mentions that the skip is to be held in it's point of origin in a pharma company. It then says that it's in a vault. In a site. The site bit I can let slide because the Foundation occasionally does make pre-existing facilities into Sites but I feel that the whole "vault" thing seems awfully convenient. This may just be me but I don't think a pharma company has the money or the reach to covertly build a super-secure vault. And don't start with the "The Foundation built it after it was found" thing because if this thing is as dangerous as it is described, I doubt it would have stayed in an inferior vault for so long and even if it did, it likes it's prey living. Foundation guys coming in is the equivalent of them saying "Hey monster-mate! We want you locked up forever but for some reason we'll send in a metric fuckton of our guys for you to messily devour!"

I also don't really see how giving it a D-Class every week contains it. Doesn't it get hungry otherwise? The term "prey" typically means something being killed for food. And in the next sentence, you say that not doing this will result in a breach. How does giving it one guy every seven days satisfy you if your a water-monster that can seemingly break out at any moment and get hundreds of guys?

+ The Odds and Ends
- Title for hiding text

It seems that several people had attempted to break out through the front-door, despite the security measures rendering the site inescapable. As they are all face-down, you do not recognize the bodies in the water.

What? The 2316 shoutout makes no sense. Isn't it only affecting this one lake somewhere? And evem if it wasn't, why would the would the Foundation keep a water-based anomaly in the same site as a water-manipulating one?

you're still Level 0, and thus, effectively worthless as far as Overwatch is concerned.

This isn't a crit but whenever I hear the word overwatch referring to the 05's, I honestly just think of Tracer saying "Secure, Contain, Pharah!" in her stupid British accent.

MTF Eta-9 ("See No Evil") will be immediately dispatched to contain the threat

How would Eta-10 be useful? From what I can tell, it's not a visual-based anomaly.

In conclusion, my feelings towards the skip are that it heavily suffers from "It's pretty decent buuuuut someone's already done it better."syndrome. the fact that it was you makes the problem a bit more glaring for me, actually. This skip, while a cool use of the format screw (We need more first-person horror passages in the wiki) is pretty much a discount version of your 001 entry. It has a similar premise, a similar style and overall just kinda feels like the 001 but missing some of the soul that made your proposal one of the best articles on the site (in my opinion).

Sorry if I was too harsh. Sometimes I feel like my reviews can be a bit mean-spirited unintentionally on my part.

- The Scary Name Man

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It is very well written, that can't be argued. The color effects of the guy passing out during the reading is a nice touch. But there are some serious problems for me.

First, the format isn't proper for an SCP entry. It isn't just a play with the format, it is outright a tale, just with a character reading an SCP entry in the middle of it. It is fair in context, but belong elsewhere in the site.

Second, excessive danger. The point is, this thing being able to cause an apocalypse doesn't add to the story being told. This is a very good first person narrative of a Containment Breach being witnessed by someone with very weak grasp of what is going on, then acquiring access to sensible information, but only when it is too late. That certainly demands at least a sensible portion of the site being isolated, not only to trap our protagonist, but also to give her a chance to rob data without worrying with further consequences. But it hardly require that the danger in question be able to end the entire planet.

The entire thing just seems cheesy with the XK scenario. How the Foundation was able to find this thing in time to prevent it? How they found how to deal with this before it came to be impossible to handle? How could the world survive with things like that simply appearing out of nowhere? Those are questions that it raises and that put the suspension of disbelief in check. And it doesn't matter if you have an answer to them or not. The problem is that those questions aren't even related to the actual narrative, instead they digress from it.

Keeping it Euclid, it could do its stuff without growing, or having a limiter to its growth, still high enough to warrant a site lock-down to stop a containment breach. Being composed of water, it could have a better containment procedure than "keep the water-Minotaur at the center of the labyrinth", like a water-proof sealed vault, and still have enough of a chance of it being compromised to justify the narrative. Simply having it hunting humans and being sapient and hard to distinguish from normal water would certainly be enough for the Foundation to prefer to lose some assets with this lock-down than to risk its total escape along with whatever it thinks to get from the site.

By the way, the weekly sacrifice of a D-Class seems senseless here. If it prefers living prey and hates all forms of life, it would be simpler to throw animals there. Even more if it needs an alarm strapped on the prey to even find it in the vault.

item.title: For Victory We Ride, Fury of the Storm!
item.content:encoded: Very nicely done, especially with it being made at the last minute. I don't know if it was intentional or not, but I like how the pictures move away from depicting lightning as it's revealed that the water is the focus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, I guess it's just that one.

But given the overall structure of this versus your 001, it seems like you've found a gimmick that works and are now writing it into the ground. That's how it comes off, anyway, hence the disappointment. I'm probably the only person who cares though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A very well-put-together tale! I like how the SCP infection is foreshadowed in the beginning with the implication that the SCP's shouldn't have changed. +1 from me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a couple of things that bug me here: the pictures being different between the level 0 and level 2 article and having no obvious relation to the skip, and the implication that the missing text in the description is from the POV character getting woozy feels like a cop-out. All that said this is still an effective skip, and the mix of prose and traditional SCP document works just as well as it did in your 001 proposal. So have a semi-reluctant upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ok, I enjoyed this, I think it's an excellently well-paced piece and is a good example of execution that elevates an average concept to the next level. However, I honestly can't brook anymore of these kinds of tale-heavy works as SCP articles. This directly goes against the primary strength of the scip format, namely the sole use of formal documents to tell a story. As this contest has shown, restrictions can produce great works by forcing people to creatively adapt to said restrictions, coming up with things that they usually won't think of, just as the SCP format should (ideally) do.

Rant aside, I'd just like to reiterate that this is an excellent piece.

item.title: Re: Shaggydredlocks is an actual god
item.content:encoded: It is. You can check it easily.

item.title: Shaggydredlocks is an actual god
item.content:encoded: Haven't read this yet, but if it's from the same person that made the S. D. Locke proposal I can assure myself it's gonna be great.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
greyerman, as per the Guide for Newbies, Site Behavior tab:

In-character Behavior: Don't. Yes, you've read Duke Till Dawn or The War of the Doctors, and many of the characters in that are avatars of writers here. But we don't talk like that on the forums. Everything on the site and in the chat is out-of-character, meaning you are to write as if you understand that this is a fiction site and we are writers of fiction.

Please don't sign posts off as if you're trying to indicate that you're an MTF member. We're not a roleplay site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really love your writing ability, and the SCP excerpts are great.

For some reason I find myself really disliking the format, but for what you were going for, I don't know of a different way you could have achieved that. The style of SCP-2204 might have worked better… The white text portions near the end however, nice touch.

+1 from me, moving on to your SCP-3980

Grey
MTF Iota-10

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was written pretty well, and it was pretty easy to feel more connected to the narrator, which I think is a thing that happens often when people try and write with the 2nd person narrative. But I think the concept is a bit too similar to your 001 proposal. Also, I liked your 2316 reference. Overall, I enjoyed it, but the concept was too much like your 001 that it didn't really grab me. As of right now, novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, I didn't like it at first. "Dark and stormy night" made me immediately assume that this SCP was a thing that could only be written in the style of cliche ghost stories, however as I slowly got more immersed in the character of this level 0 employee I began to feel an overwhelming sense of dread. Everything began to feel real as you viscerally described the situation this poor character was in. The way you showed the greying and disappearing of text really added credence to this. This is an incredibly disturbing and creative concept and that stinger at the end? As a long time fan of creepy pastas, that made me feel both nostalgic and afraid to go to the drinking fountain.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The prose and narrative is outstanding. I think I prefer it to the 001, due to brevity.

My two nitpicks are continuity-based.

Firstly, shouldn't something that's only provisionally contained, with the capacity for world-changing scenarios, be classed as Keter?

Secondly, as of the point that the in-narrative article was written, the entity has not made contact with the outside, yet the photos depict a storm cloud. Is that picture of the cloud intended to be interpreted as taken in the subterranean cavern?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really nice skip, though the integration of a kind of tale/SCP document is something I both like and don't. +1 no less.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aha, I read this a couple days ago when someone asked for format-screw SCPs specifically, over on the subreddit. I really like the writing style, especially using white text to simulate momentary black-outs as the character is reading. The main thing I started thinking about though, was how did it not contaminate Earth's water system prior to containment by the Foundation?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I originally couldn't figure out what this was supposed to be, so I downvoted. Now that I understand, I like this a lot more. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm going to go against the grain and say I reached the end feeling pretty disappointed. I'm a huge fan of end-of-the-world-scenarios (Like your 001 proposal, boy do I love that one) but at no point did I find myself properly immersed. The fact that this takes from both the format and concepts of your 001 proposal but doesn't really deviate too far from either is something I wish I could look past.

As it stands, I'll be no-voting this one, because despite my complaints above, damn do I wish I could write as well as you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I will never look at rain the same way again. Yeah, thanks for that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh man I was enjoying this from the start but that gut punch at the end got me good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It works, but it's very similar to your 001 proposal, both as a concept and in its storytelling. The core concept of this SCP borrows a lot from your 001; it's a blob-based creature that absorbs similar matter to increase its size, it was created by some vague weather-related disaster (solar flares/explosion in 001, but not exactly sure what prompted the creation of 3280, guessing some kind of bizarre rainstorm judging by the images), it wants(?) all humans to die, and it seems like they'll succeed in doing so soon. As for the narrative, it's essentially cut from the same cloth, and I feel like in direct comparison, 3280 feels rather cut down, and while the atmosphere is there, it doesn't quite have the ambiguity your 001 did (what was up with that "dog" and black hand in 001 anyway?).

I originally wanted to upvote this, and if I hadn't previously read your 001, I would've as this is well written, but I feel like this SCP is more or less just a repainted version of that, and thus kind of a creative blunder.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is quite an interesting article. I would like to point out that See No Evil appears to be mislabeled as Eta-9, unless I'm missing something. Nice 2316 reference too. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yet more proof that dihydrogen monoxide needs to be banned. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this always happens to me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It is a dark and stormy night. You've spent the last several hours hiding in the broom closet with naught but the rank water of the mop-sink to sustain you. The chaos has long since died down. It's time to make a break for it. You slowly open the door.

That's when you see him…

Shia LaBeouf

In all seriousness, this was wonderfully atmospheric and handled the theme of Day 3 very well. I also have to say, despite this being a rip off of your own 001 you make the format unique enough here so as to have this be its own animal, and not just 001 but water.

Kudos shaggy.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So the Waters of Mars from Doctor Who managed to reach Earth? Except, you made it seem even more terrifying than Doctor Who did.
Bringing on the thunda'!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh my god thats great

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: you know he said this was last minute imagine what he could do with a longer time to make it, holycrap

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure how I'm ripping off a bunch of other articles here, tropes being tools and all, could you be more specific? I suppose I can see the shoutout to SCP-2316 coming off like that, but it's just a reference; it's got no conceptual overlap.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No, I'm sorry. I was reading this, going, "It's okay, but shaggydredlock's proposal did it better and/or first", and then I find out you wrote this too? And outwardly rip off a bunch of other articles? I'm incredibly disappointed.

EDIT: And I say this as someone who literally rewrote multiple existing articles and jammed them together into something 'new'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
By their catastrophes combined, I AM CAPTAIN DREADLOCK!

…yknow. If the entire planet turns on us, if Earth becomes a keter-class SCP, that's gonna be just absolutely peachy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
or earth

fire

water

air

Long ago, the scp wiki lived together in harmony. Then, everything changed when the jam contest attacked. Only the dreadlocks, master of all four apocalyscps, could stop them, but when the memez needed him most, he vanished. A seventy two hours passed and my brother and I discovered the new dreadlocks, an writer named shaggy. And although his writing skills are great, he has a lot to learn before he's ready to post anything. But I believe shaggy can destroy the world.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait, this was "last minute"? I loved this one!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whats next, the air will turn on us?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First the sun turned against us and now all the water? Your first person apocalypse SCPs are really fantastic.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My complaints that I had from your 001 have arisen here again. I do not believe that prose belongs in an SCP, unless there's an explainable reason why. And since this doesn't have the same weight and emotion that carried your 001, I cannot upvote this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's actually Modulum's

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh you and your dank copypasta yossi

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You ripped off your own 001 proposal yet I still love it. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: a baby was born

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When Water Broke

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's a certain form of writing that you're just the absolute master of.

STOP BEING SO GOOD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hoooly shit did I not think I would finish this in time!

Thanks to Hippo, Kakroom, and CaptainKirby for some crucial last-minute feedback!

ETA: Also, thanks to tretter and Hippo for brainstorming help!

ETA 2: I feel as though I should give a shoutout to the article which helped form the initial idea of this particular style to begin with. In addition to ripping off myself, both this and my 001 are ripping off a single line (the collapsible) from SCP-2204.

If you like this, you can find more on my author page.

ETA 3 creds: First image can be found here and is in the Public Domain.

Second image is from GlaicierNPF, taken by David Restivo, and is also in the Public Domain.
